The white star is at [9, 16] with 6 pixels. So clicking the orange square moved the white star to [9, 16] (near the orange square at [11, 16]).
The purple square jumped from [35, 44] back to [16, 32] on the orange trail. This is similar to what happened before when I clicked the orange square - the purple square jumped to the orange trail.
So the pattern is:
- Clicking a colored square moves the white star to that square and pulls objects from other trails to join that trail.
- Clicking a ring/diamond pulls objects on its trail toward it.
Now I understand the game better. The goal might be to:
1. Get both colored squares to their respective rings
2. Or get all objects to one ring

- Purple square at [16, 32] on the orange trail (between orange square and gray diamond)
- White star at [9, 16] near the orange square
- Orange diamond at bottom
- Purple ring at top right
I need to:

2. Move the purple square to the purple ring
But the purple square is now on the orange trail. To get it to the purple ring, I need to click the purple ring first, which would pull the purple square to the purple trail, then click the purple ring again to pull it closer.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider. The purple square is on the orange trail now. If I click the purple ring, will it pull the purple square to the purple trail? Let me try.
